store.name: BookHaven
store.description: Your ultimate destination for books of every genre

books.title: The Midnight Library

author.name: Matt Haig
author.bio: Matt Haig is a British author for children and adults. His work has been translated into over 40 languages.

books.description: Between life and death there is a library, and within that library, the shelves go on forever. Every book provides a chance to try another life you could have lived.
books.about: A dazzling novel about all the choices that go into a life well lived, set in a magical library that exists between life and death. Nora Seed finds herself in the Midnight Library, where she has the chance to undo every regret and try out any of the other lives she might have lived — discovering what it truly means to be alive.

books.title: Atomic Habits

author.name: James Clear
author.bio: James Clear is an author and speaker focused on habits, decision-making, and continuous improvement. His work has appeared in the New York Times, Forbes, and Time magazine.

books.description: No matter your goals, Atomic Habits offers a proven framework for improving every day. James Clear reveals practical strategies that will teach you exactly how to form good habits, break bad ones.
books.about: A groundbreaking guide to building good habits and breaking bad ones. Using a framework called the Four Laws of Behavior Change, Clear explains how tiny changes in behavior can lead to remarkable results. Packed with evidence-based strategies, this book is the definitive guide to breaking bad behaviors and sticking to good ones.

books.title: Dune

author.name: Frank Herbert
author.bio: Frank Herbert was an American science fiction author best known for the novel Dune and its five sequels. He was also a short story author and worked as a newspaper journalist, photographer, and ecology lecturer.

books.description: Set in the distant future amidst a feudal interstellar society, Dune tells the story of young Paul Atreides, whose family accepts stewardship of the desert planet Arrakis.
books.about: Dune is a 1965 epic science fiction novel set in the far future in a feudal interstellar society. The story explores themes of politics, religion, ecology, technology, and human emotion, as the forces of the Empire confront each other on a desert planet. It won the Hugo Award and the inaugural Nebula Award for Best Novel.

books.title: The Psychology of Money

author.name: Morgan Housel
author.bio: Morgan Housel is a partner at The Collaborative Fund and a former columnist at The Motley Fool and The Wall Street Journal.

books.description: Timeless lessons on wealth, greed, and happiness. Doing well with money isn't necessarily about what you know. It's about how you behave.
books.about: A collection of 19 short stories exploring the strange ways people think about money and teaches you how to make better sense of one of life's most important topics. The book explains that financial success is not a hard science but a soft skill, where how you behave matters more than what you know.

books.title: To Kill a Mockingbird

author.name: Harper Lee
author.bio: Harper Lee was an American novelist best known for her 1960 novel 'To Kill a Mockingbird', which won the Pulitzer Prize in 1961 and became a landmark of American literature.

books.description: The unforgettable novel of a childhood in a sleepy Southern town and the crisis of conscience that rocked it. A story of compassion, courage, and justice.
books.about: Set in the fictional town of Maycomb, Alabama, during the Depression, this Pulitzer Prize-winning masterpiece tells the story of young Scout Finch and her father Atticus, a lawyer who defends a Black man unjustly accused of rape. An enduring classic that confronts issues of racial injustice and moral growth.

books.title: Sapiens: A Brief History of Humankind

author.name: Yuval Noah Harari
author.bio: Yuval Noah Harari is an Israeli public intellectual, historian and professor in the Department of History at the Hebrew University of Jerusalem. He is the author of the popular science bestsellers Sapiens, Homo Deus, and 21 Lessons for the 21st Century.

books.description: From a renowned historian comes a groundbreaking narrative of humanity's creation and evolution that explores how biology and history have defined us and enhanced our understanding of what it means to be human.
books.about: Sapiens surveys the entire length of human history, from the very first humans to walk the earth to the radical and sometimes devastating breakthroughs of the Cognitive, Agricultural, and Scientific Revolutions. Drawing on insights from biology, anthropology, paleontology, and economics, Harari explores how the currents of history have shaped our human societies, the animals and plants around us, and even our personalities.

books.title: The Alchemist

author.name: Paulo Coelho
author.bio: Paulo Coelho is a Brazilian lyricist and novelist, best known for his novel 'The Alchemist'. In 2014, he uploaded his personal papers online to create a virtual Paulo Coelho Foundation.

books.description: A special 25th anniversary edition of the extraordinary international bestseller, including a new Foreword by Paulo Coelho. Every few decades a book is published that changes the lives of its readers forever.
books.about: The Alchemist follows the journey of an Andalusian shepherd boy named Santiago who yearns to travel in search of a worldly treasure. His quest will lead him to riches far different — and far more satisfying — than he ever imagined. Along the way, Santiago meets a Romani woman, a man who calls himself king, and an alchemist, all of whom point him in the direction of his quest.

books.title: Harry Potter and the Sorcerer's Stone

author.name: J.K. Rowling
author.bio: J.K. Rowling is the author of the much-loved series of seven Harry Potter novels, initially published between 1997 and 2007. She also writes fiction for adults and, under the pen name Robert Galbraith, is the author of the Cormoran Strike crime series.

books.description: Harry Potter has never been the star of a Quidditch team, scoring points while riding a broom far above the ground. He knows no spells, has never helped to hatch a dragon, and has never worn a cloak of invisibility.
books.about: Harry Potter's life is miserable. His parents are dead and he's stuck with his heartless relatives, who force him to live in a tiny closet under the stairs. But his fortune changes when he receives a letter that tells him the truth about himself: he's a wizard. A mischievous poltergeist, a three-headed dog, and a dangerous quest for a mystical stone await Harry at Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry.

books.title: The 48 Laws of Power

author.name: Robert Greene
author.bio: Robert Greene is an American author known for his books on strategy, power, and seduction. He has written several international bestsellers, including The 48 Laws of Power, The Art of Seduction, and Mastery.

books.description: Amoral, cunning, ruthless, and instructive, this multi-million-copy New York Times bestseller is the definitive manual for anyone interested in gaining, observing, or defending against ultimate control.
books.about: Drawing from thousands of years of the history of power, author Robert Greene distills the world's wisdom on power into 48 essential laws. This book is a fascinating chronicle and distillation of the philosophies of Machiavelli, Sun Tzu, Carl von Clausewitz and other great thinkers. Essential reading for understanding the dynamics of power in business, politics, and everyday life.

books.title: Think and Grow Rich

author.name: Napoleon Hill
author.bio: Napoleon Hill was an American self-help author. He is known best for his book Think and Grow Rich, which is among the 10 best-selling self-help books of all time.

books.description: This is the original 1937 edition of Napoleon Hill's classic book on achieving success, Think and Grow Rich. This book has been referred to as the 'Granddaddy of All Motivational Literature.'
books.about: Think and Grow Rich was written and published during the Great Depression. Based on Hill's earlier work The Law of Success, it was written following interviews with many successful people of his time. It distills 13 principles for accumulating wealth derived from the study of over 500 of the most successful people in American history, including Andrew Carnegie, Thomas Edison, and Henry Ford.

books.title: The Silent Patient

author.name: Alex Michaelides
author.bio: Alex Michaelides was born in Cyprus and educated at Cambridge University and the American Film Institute. The Silent Patient is his debut novel.

books.description: Alicia Berenson's life is seemingly perfect. A famous painter married to an in-demand fashion photographer, she lives in a grand house with big windows overlooking a park in one of London's most desirable areas.
books.about: The Silent Patient is a shocking psychological thriller of a woman's act of violence against her husband — and of the therapist obsessed with uncovering her motive. One of the most anticipated debuts of 2019, it has become one of the most talked about thrillers of the decade. A jaw-dropping twist that you won't see coming.

books.title: Educated

author.name: Tara Westover
author.bio: Tara Westover is an American author. Born in Idaho to a survivalist family, she did not attend school. She self-studied and passed the ACT, gained admission to Brigham Young University, and earned a PhD from Cambridge.

books.description: An unforgettable memoir about a young girl who, kept out of school, leaves her survivalist family and goes on to earn a PhD from Cambridge University.
books.about: Educated is the story of Tara Westover's extraordinary journey from living off the grid in rural Idaho to earning a PhD from Cambridge University, despite never attending school as a child. It is a story about the power of education to open doors, and about the difficulty of leaving family behind, even a dangerous one.
